metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'cleanly-closed tickets' precisely in the plan (e.g., 'tickets tagged `verdict=PASS` at close with no subsequent reopen event in the E1 corpus, identified by field X') so AC3's computation is deterministic and reproducible.
evidence: Plan uses 'cleanly-closed tickets' without defining what makes a ticket 'cleanly closed' (e.g., a specific corpus label, a field value, human triage flag, or absence of a reopen event)
No reference to a parent epic, ADR, or corpus schema field that supplies this definition
'Cleanly-closed' is the denominator of the <=2% gate; misidentifying it produces a meaningless or misleading gate result
findings.finding: 'Cleanly-closed tickets' is used in AC3 as the denominator for the primary <=2% gate metric but is never defined in the plan — an executor cannot identify which tickets qualify without an external definition or a reference to one.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'The PASS->BLOCK flip rate on cleanly-closed tickets is computed and recorded per candidate'
scenarios: Executor filters tickets by a different criterion than intended, computing a flip rate over the wrong population and producing a gate signal that cannot be compared across candidates or validated
Two executors independently compute different rates because the definition was unspecified
findings.suggested_fix: Add a definition sentence to AC3: 'Cleanly-closed tickets are defined as reviews in the E1 corpus whose final recorded verdict was PASS with no subsequent reopen or escalation flag (corpus field: `status=closed_clean`).' Adjust field name to match actual corpus schema.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'cleanly-closed tickets' precisely in the plan (e.g., as `post_claim_edit_class='none' AND reopen_count=0` in the E1 corpus, or an explicit sidecar join criterion) so the AC3 flip-rate denominator is unambiguous.
evidence: Search for 'cleanly-closed' across repo returned zero hits.
Search for 'cleanly closed' returned zero hits matching this usage.
The E1 outcome corpus schema has `post_claim_edit_class` and `reopen_count` as the closest structural proxies, but the plan never maps 'cleanly-closed' to these fields.
ADR 0036 does not define 'cleanly-closed tickets'.
findings.finding: The plan uses the term 'cleanly-closed tickets' as the denominator for the primary <=2% PASS->BLOCK gate metric (Why section and AC3), but this term is never defined anywhere in the plan, the codebase, or the named ADR — no grep or read probe finds a definition of 'cleanly-closed'.
findings.location: Why section: 'the epic's gate requires <=2% PASS->BLOCK flips on cleanly-closed tickets'; AC3: 'PASS->BLOCK flip rate on cleanly-closed tickets'
scenarios: An implementer computing the flip rate cannot identify the denominator population without a definition, potentially producing incomparable flip-rate figures across candidates.

If 'cleanly-closed' means post_claim_edit_class='none' AND reopen_count=0, the flip rate will differ from a definition based on completion_verifier_fail_count=0 — the choice materially changes whether the <=2% gate passes.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a definitions subsection stating: 'A ticket is cleanly closed if it satisfies post_claim_edit_class=="none" AND reopen_count==0 AND force_close==false in the E1 outcome corpus.' Align this with the <=2% gate row's stated intent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'baseline verdict source' specification to the plan: either (a) join the most-recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar per ticket (filtered to the relevant IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION), or (b) execute a control run on `main` before the candidate run, and commit both runs' outputs.
evidence: outcome_corpus.jsonl schema (probed): no verdict/PASS/BLOCK column.

sidecar.py:482 persists 'verdict' from each live review run, so each REVIEW_RESULT sidecar carries a verdict — but the plan does not state that these sidecars serve as the baseline, nor how to handle tickets with multiple sidecar rounds.
No 'baseline_verdict' field or 'control_run' step is described anywhere in the plan text.
findings.finding: The transition matrix requires a BASELINE verdict (the PASS/BLOCK verdict the current/pre-candidate gate produced for each sampled ticket), but the plan names no source for this baseline column. The E1 outcome corpus contains no verdict field; the plan does not specify whether baseline verdicts come from existing REVIEW_RESULT sidecars, a fresh control run, or another artifact.
findings.location: What section: 'compute the verdict-transition matrix (PASS->PASS/PASS->BLOCK/BLOCK->PASS/BLOCK->BLOCK)'; AC2: 'commits the verdict-transition matrix'
scenarios: Without a specified baseline source, an implementer may run a fresh control pass (doubling LLM cost) or use an arbitrary historical sidecar — producing different baseline populations.

If the most-recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar is used as the baseline, tickets reviewed with different IMPACT_MODEL_VERSIONs will be mixed unless filtered — exactly the contamination ADR 0036 warns against.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how baseline verdicts are obtained for each sample ticket (e.g., a separate run of the production/main-branch build, or retrieval from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars filtered to the correct IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION) so the transition matrix can be computed as a (baseline, candidate) diff.
evidence: E1 outcome_corpus.jsonl rows contain {ticket_id, ticket_type, level, post_claim_edit_class, reopen_count, force_close, completion_verifier_fail_count, review_round_count, had_persisted_review} — no `verdict` field.

The plan states the replay 'executes the candidate branch build over the full sample' but is silent on where the baseline (pre-candidate) verdict comes from — a second run against the production build, or from stored REVIEW_RESULT sidecars.
Without baseline verdicts the transition matrix is uncomputable: you only have candidate verdicts, not the (before, after) pairs needed to classify PASS->BLOCK etc.
findings.finding: The verdict-transition matrix (PASS->PASS / PASS->BLOCK / BLOCK->PASS / BLOCK->BLOCK) requires a baseline verdict per ticket, but the E1 outcome_corpus.jsonl has no `verdict` field, and the plan only describes executing the *candidate* branch build over the sample — the source of the baseline (pre-candidate) verdict is unspecified.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'The replay executes the candidate branch build over the full sample and commits the verdict-transition matrix'
scenarios: Implementer runs the candidate build and has 200 candidate verdicts but no baseline verdicts to diff against — the transition matrix cannot be constructed.

If baseline is sourced from historical REVIEW_RESULT sidecars, the sidecars may predate IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION plan-v3 and ADR 0036 mandates they not be pooled across versions — creating a version-mixing hazard the plan does not address.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit step: 'Run the production (main-branch) build over the same ~200-ticket sample first to establish baseline verdicts; then run the candidate build and diff (baseline_verdict, candidate_verdict) per ticket, segmented by IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION. Both runs must use the same IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION cohort to satisfy ADR 0036 non-pooling.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address LLM nondeterminism: either pin temperature=0 in the replay driver (via cfg override to the batch runner), reference the existing checkpoint seam as a way to hold the baseline fixed, or bound the expected variance floor and document it as a known uncertainty in the AC4 writeup.
evidence: Plan says 'Live LLM calls (billable): the replay runs the real pipeline' with no mention of temperature pinning or replication runs
The eval harnesses (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml etc.) all pin `temperature: 0` for determinism; no such pinning is named for the replay driver

AC3: 'The PASS->BLOCK flip rate on cleanly-closed tickets is computed and recorded per candidate (the input consumed by R1's <=2% gate row)' — a 2% ceiling on ~200 tickets is ≤4 flips, a very small margin for noise
findings.finding: The replay runs live LLM calls with no stated strategy for controlling LLM nondeterminism between the candidate run and the baseline. The plan's primary gate criterion is a PASS→BLOCK flip rate ≤2% on a ~200-ticket sample. Without temperature control or replication, natural sampling variance across two runs of the same model on the same input can produce a non-trivial number of verdict flips that are attributable to the LLM's stochastic output rather than the candidate change — potentially causing a good candidate to fail the 2% gate or a bad one to pass it.
scenarios: On a 200-ticket sample with ~2% natural LLM variance per verdict, ~4 flips occur from sampling noise; a candidate that introduces 0 genuine flips fails the gate
A candidate that introduces 2 genuine flips passes if LLM noise cancels them out on that specific run

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the baseline-verdict source explicitly (e.g., 'recover the most-recent PASS/BLOCK verdict from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar on each sampled ticket' or 'run a baseline pass of the current branch over the same sample before the candidate run') and specify how INDETERMINATE baselines are handled in the matrix.
evidence: Plan: 'compute the verdict-transition matrix (PASS->PASS/PASS->BLOCK/BLOCK->PASS/BLOCK->BLOCK)'

docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md schema table: fields are ticket_id, ticket_type, level, post_claim_edit_class, reopen_count, force_close, completion_verifier_fail_count, review_round_count, had_persisted_review — no `verdict` field
ABSENCE: no baseline verdict source or baseline-run protocol is specified anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The verdict-transition matrix (PASS->PASS / PASS->BLOCK / BLOCK->PASS / BLOCK->BLOCK) is a core output, but the plan never defines where the BASELINE verdict per ticket comes from. The E1 outcome_corpus.jsonl schema (confirmed in runs/README.md) has no `verdict` field — only post_claim_edit_class, reopen_count, force_close, review_round_count, etc. Without specifying whether the baseline is (a) recovered from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecars already stamped on each ticket, (b) a separate baseline run of the current gate over the sample, or (c) some other source, an executor cannot compute the matrix.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'commits the verdict-transition matrix'
scenarios: Executor runs the candidate branch over the sample and obtains new verdicts, then cannot determine which cell of the matrix each ticket falls into without the baseline verdict.
If the baseline is inferred from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars, those may have INDETERMINATE verdicts that do not fit the PASS/BLOCK matrix schema.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Baseline acquisition' sub-step to the replay method: run the current (unmodified) gate over the ~200 sampled tickets first, store each verdict as `baseline_verdict ∈ {PASS, BLOCK}`, and commit this as the baseline column. Alternatively, recover verdicts from the most-recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar per ticket and define a mapping of INDETERMINATE→excluded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'cleanly-closed tickets' in terms of the outcome_corpus.jsonl schema fields (e.g., 'tickets with post_claim_edit_class=="none" AND reopen_count==0 AND force_close==False') so the AC3 denominator is unambiguous and reproducible.
evidence: Plan AC3 verbatim: 'The PASS->BLOCK flip rate on cleanly-closed tickets is computed and recorded per candidate (the input consumed by R1's <=2% gate row).'

docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md post_claim_edit_class closed vocabulary: {plan-authored-post-claim, operator-attested-retag, ac-strengthened, substantive-unclassified, cosmetic, none} — no 'cleanly-closed' value
ABSENCE: 'cleanly-closed' does not appear in ADR 0036, the outcome corpus schema, or any linked artifact
findings.finding: 'Cleanly-closed tickets' is used in AC3 as the denominator for the primary <=2% PASS->BLOCK flip rate, but the term is never defined in the plan. The outcome_corpus.jsonl has a closed `post_claim_edit_class` vocabulary (plan-authored-post-claim, operator-attested-retag, ac-strengthened, substantive-unclassified, cosmetic, none) plus `reopen_count` and `force_close`, but 'cleanly-closed' maps to none of these values unambiguously. An executor cannot identify which tickets qualify as the denominator.
findings.location: AC3: 'The PASS->BLOCK flip rate on cleanly-closed tickets is computed and recorded per candidate'
scenarios: Executor interprets 'cleanly-closed' as post_claim_edit_class=='none' → denominator is ~576 tickets; another interprets it as reopen_count==0 AND force_close==False → a different, larger set. The reported flip rate is not reproducible.
The <=2% threshold in R1's gate row is tied to this exact denominator; a wrong definition silently changes the decision criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'cleanly-closed tickets' with an explicit filter expression, e.g.: 'tickets where post_claim_edit_class == "none" AND reopen_count == 0 AND force_close == False (no substantive post-claim edits, no reopens, no force-closes).' Commit the filter as part of the replay driver so the denominator is encoded, not inferred.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'per-criterion finding delta' schema and computation rule (e.g., for each criterion: baseline_count, candidate_count, net_delta, and optionally new/removed finding fingerprints) and co-locate it with the verdict-transition matrix output spec.
evidence: Plan AC2: 'commits the verdict-transition matrix segmented by IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION, with per-criterion finding deltas'
Plan What: 'Record per-criterion finding deltas alongside verdict flips so an R-row's churn is attributable'
ABSENCE: no schema, format, or computation rule for 'per-criterion finding deltas' is defined anywhere in the plan
production_batch_runner.py outputs: {findings: [...], criteria_count: int, batch_plan: dict} — no delta computation
findings.finding: The plan requires 'per-criterion finding deltas' in AC2 but never defines the delta schema. The production_batch_runner emits a findings list and a batch_plan coverage dict per run; the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar stores per-criterion finding counts and fingerprints. But 'delta' is ambiguous: it could mean (a) count(new findings per criterion) − count(baseline findings per criterion), (b) a set-diff by finding fingerprint, (c) a boolean flip per criterion, or (d) something else. No output format or co-located processing rule ties this to the transition matrix schema.
findings.location: AC2: 'with per-criterion finding deltas' / What section: 'Record per-criterion finding deltas alongside verdict flips'
scenarios: Two executors implement different delta definitions and produce non-comparable outputs; the attributability claim in the plan ('churn is attributable') cannot be verified.
A pure count delta hides finding-text changes that don't change count; a fingerprint set-diff requires a stable fingerprinting scheme that may not match across branch builds.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback rule for INDETERMINATE replay outcomes (e.g., 'INDETERMINATE results are excluded from the matrix and flip-rate denominator, and their count is reported separately in the writeup') so the AC3 metric is unambiguous.
evidence: Plan: 'the replay executes the candidate branch build over the full sample' — no failure-handling protocol stated
src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py lines 134–212: three INDETERMINATE degradation paths for LLMUnavailableError, verify failure, and finder failure
ABSENCE: the plan does not specify whether INDETERMINATE-verdict tickets are excluded from the matrix, counted as PASS, counted as BLOCK, or reported in a separate column
findings.finding: The replay runs the real pipeline (live LLM calls) over ~200 tickets, but the plan specifies no fallback protocol for individual ticket runs that produce an INDETERMINATE verdict (LLMUnavailableError, mid-run failure, partial run). The gate_dispatch code has three explicit INDETERMINATE degradation paths (LLMUnavailableError, verify failure, finder failure). Without a defined handling rule, the flip-rate denominator and matrix cell counts are ambiguous when any sample tickets fail.
findings.location: What section / AC2–AC3: matrix and flip rate computation
scenarios: 3 of 200 tickets produce INDETERMINATE on the candidate run due to a transient LLM error; one executor excludes them, another counts them as BLOCK, producing different flip rates that straddle the 2% threshold.
A systematic outage during the candidate run degrades many tickets to INDETERMINATE; without a handling rule the matrix is silently underpopulated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line protocol to the What section: 'INDETERMINATE verdicts (LLM outage or mid-run failure) are excluded from the matrix and the flip-rate computation; the exclusion count is reported per candidate run.' Record this in the replay driver so it is enforced, not left to the analyst.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the stratification allocation rule in the plan (e.g., 'proportional to E1 corpus distribution with a minimum of N=5 per non-empty stratum cell, collapsing cells below minimum') so the sample draw is deterministic and reproducible.
evidence: 'Stratification: ticket type, overlay triggers, verdict, model' states the dimensions but not the allocation rule

With four strata dimensions the cross-product of cells can be large; without an allocation rule the executor may produce a sample that under-represents rare strata or over-represents common ones, affecting the validity of the segmented matrix
findings.finding: The stratification method is not specified: the plan lists four strata dimensions (ticket type, overlay triggers, verdict, model) but does not say how to allocate the ~200 samples across them (proportional to corpus distribution, equal per stratum, or fixed minimum per cell). This is a defaultable but real methodological decision.
findings.location: What section: 'Stratification: ticket type, overlay triggers, verdict, model.'
scenarios: Executor uses proportional allocation, producing near-zero samples for rare overlay-trigger + BLOCK cells; the segmented matrix has empty or single-sample cells that cannot support the gate decision

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the baseline verdict source for the transition matrix: either (a) the persisted REVIEW_RESULT sidecar `verdict` for each sampled ticket (cheapest, but requires acknowledging that post-claim-edited tickets have evolved text, or filtering them from the denominator) or (b) a separate fresh baseline re-run, with the same ticket-text-snapshot strategy used for both baseline and candidate runs.
evidence: Plan says 'compute the verdict-transition matrix (PASS->PASS/PASS->BLOCK/BLOCK->PASS/BLOCK->BLOCK)' but names no source for the baseline column
outcome_corpus.jsonl schema (runs/README.md) has no `verdict` field — baseline verdicts must come from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars or a fresh re-run
sidecar.py line 482: `'verdict': verdict.get('verdict')` confirms REVIEW_RESULT sidecars do carry the gate verdict
`assemble_context` calls `_reads.show_ticket(ticket_id)` on the live store, reading CURRENT ticket text not the text at original-review time
outcome_corpus README §5 re-verification: 8.3% of reviewed work tickets have `ac-strengthened` or higher post-claim-edit-class, meaning their current text differs from the reviewed text
findings.finding: The transition matrix requires a 'before' (baseline) verdict for each ticket, but the plan never specifies its source. The candidate re-run calls `assemble_context(req.target_ticket)` which reads the CURRENT live ticket text; if the baseline comes from persisted REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (which captured the verdict at original-review time), tickets with post-claim edits (~8.3% of reviewed work tickets) will have different plan text in the two columns — inflating spurious PASS→BLOCK flips unrelated to the candidate change. If instead the baseline is a separate fresh re-run of the current (non-candidate) build, the cost doubles and LLM nondeterminism is compounded across both runs.
findings.location: What section / AC2 ('executes the candidate branch build over the full sample and commits the verdict-transition matrix')
scenarios: A ticket reviewed months ago as PASS has since had its AC block strengthened; the candidate re-run sees the harder plan text and produces a BLOCK, recorded as a PASS→BLOCK flip — but the flip is caused by ticket evolution, not the candidate change
Baseline comes from a fresh non-candidate re-run; LLM sampling flips 2–3 verdicts at random; the flip rate exceeds the 2% gate threshold even though the candidate itself causes zero genuine flips
findings.suggested_fix: The most pragmatic approach: read baseline verdicts from persisted REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (already available, zero extra LLM cost), and restrict the 'cleanly-closed' PASS→BLOCK denominator to tickets whose `post_claim_edit_class == 'none'` so that plan-text drift cannot inflate the flip count. Document this explicitly in the method writeup (AC4).

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
no verification commands or testing plan referenced
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the strata dimensions `verdict` (PASS/BLOCK) and `model` are joinable from the persisted `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars onto the E1 outcome corpus, and specify the join key and procedure in the plan (or replace these strata with corpus-native fields if the join is not feasible).
evidence: Probed outcome_corpus.jsonl line 1: fields are ticket_id, ticket_type, level, post_claim_edit_class, reopen_count, force_close, completion_verifier_fail_count, review_round_count, had_persisted_review — no 'verdict' or 'model' fields.
docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md schema table confirms the same fields: no PASS/BLOCK verdict column, no model column.
Plan asserts stratification by 'verdict, model' — both absent from the named source corpus.
findings.finding: The plan says the stratified ~200-review sample is drawn from the E1 outcome corpus and stratified by 'ticket type, overlay triggers, verdict, model', but the E1 outcome corpus schema (probed at `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl`) contains NO `verdict` field (PASS/BLOCK) and NO `model` field — those two strata dimensions are absent from the corpus.
findings.location: What section: 'Stratification: ticket type, overlay triggers, verdict, model'; AC1: 'The stratified ~200-review sample is committed (ids + strata)'
scenarios: An implementer drawing from outcome_corpus.jsonl finds no PASS/BLOCK column and no model column, cannot satisfy the stated strata, and must either silently drop those strata or source them from a separate join — neither of which is specified.
The transition matrix's baseline-verdict column (old side of PASS->BLOCK) also has no source, since the corpus records post-claim edits rather than gate verdicts.
findings.suggested_fix: The REVIEW_RESULT sidecars persist `verdict` (line 482 of sidecar.py) and `model` (line 493). Specify a join on `ticket_id` between outcome_corpus.jsonl and the most-recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar per ticket to recover both fields; add this join to the replay driver's sampling step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC2 that asserts matrix row-sum consistency, e.g.: `jq -e '[.matrix[] | (.pass_to_pass + .pass_to_block)] | map(. == .segment_total) | all' runs/<candidate>/matrix.json` (adapt to actual schema).
evidence: AC2 says only 'commits the verdict-transition matrix' — no check that sum(PASS->PASS + PASS->BLOCK) equals total PASS tickets per segment, or that BLOCK->PASS + BLOCK->BLOCK equals total BLOCK tickets per segment
Defect taxonomy item 5: missing cardinality assertion when the criterion names a count/matrix
findings.finding: AC2 requires that the verdict-transition matrix is committed, but there is no proving command that verifies the matrix is internally consistent (e.g., that row sums equal the sample-per-segment count). Without a cardinality assertion, a silent computation bug could produce a plausible-looking but incorrect matrix.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'commits the verdict-transition matrix segmented by IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION, with per-criterion finding deltas'
scenarios: A bug in the replay driver double-counts or drops some reviews for a particular IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION segment; the matrix totals don't match the sample but no assertion catches it, and the gate decision is made on corrupt data

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify retry/backoff for individual LLM call failures, a checkpoint/resume mechanism so a partial batch run can be continued without re-processing completed items, and an explicit completion check (e.g., expected vs. actual sample count) that fails loudly before the matrix is committed.
evidence: 'Live LLM calls (billable): the replay runs the real pipeline'
AC2 requires committing the verdict-transition matrix — no mention of partial-run detection or resume
ABSENCE: no retry, backoff, checkpoint, or partial-failure surfacing specified anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan invokes ~200 live LLM calls via the production pipeline but specifies no retry/backoff for individual call failures, no checkpoint/resume for a partial batch run, and no mechanism to detect or surface partial completion — a mid-run failure could produce a truncated verdict-transition matrix that silently appears complete, invalidating the <=2% gate measurement.
findings.location: Scope section ('Live LLM calls (billable): the replay runs the real pipeline') and AC2 ('commits the verdict-transition matrix')
scenarios: LLM endpoint is rate-limited or briefly unavailable 100 calls into the 200-review batch; the remaining 100 are skipped silently; the committed matrix covers only ~50% of the sample but is indistinguishable from a full run, producing a misleading flip rate.
Replay script crashes mid-write to the runs/ directory; re-running overwrites or appends ambiguously, corrupting the transition counts.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the replay driver spec: (1) per-call retry with exponential backoff (e.g., 3 attempts); (2) append results to a per-review output file atomically so re-runs are idempotent and resume from the last uncommitted review; (3) a pre-commit assertion that len(results) == len(sample) before writing the final matrix, with a non-zero exit code on mismatch.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for the full live-pipeline re-run: state that R-candidates may change Pass-1 finder prompts (not just scoring), so the experiment must re-run the full pipeline to capture changes in what is surfaced, not only how surfaced findings are graded.
evidence: Plan says 'no overlay/config-flag mechanism, per operator direction' — names a constraint, not a fitness reason
Absent from the plan: any statement that candidates may change what Pass-1 surfaces (finder prompts), not just how findings are scored — which would justify live re-run over scoring-only replay
A scoring-only replay would be deterministic, zero additional LLM cost, and isolate the exact scoring/rubric change effect
findings.finding: The plan states the approach is 'no overlay/config-flag mechanism, per operator direction' but gives no positive fitness rationale explaining WHY a full live-pipeline re-run is the right approach for this measurement goal — specifically, why a scoring-only replay (applying rubric/gate changes to already-persisted sidecar findings without new LLM calls) would be insufficient.
scenarios: A reviewer or future planner asks why cost ~200 live LLM reviews rather than replaying existing sidecar findings; the plan has no answer
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Why or Scope section: 'A scoring-only replay (reclassifying persisted findings) cannot detect changes in what Pass-1 surfaces; since R-candidates include finder-prompt edits, the full pipeline must re-run.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
